headline: Google Dethrones OpenAI As Agencies’ Preferred AI Partner
keyNotes: Google has become the top choice for marketing agencies using artificial intelligence (AI), surpassing companies like OpenAI for the first time since 2024. This is a big shift from previous years.

Marketing agencies are no longer just picking the smartest individual artificial intelligence (AI) tools; instead, they are choosing partners that can connect all the different parts of their marketing work, like data, creative ideas, and how they reach customers.

Google is preferred because it offers a wide range of tools that work together seamlessly, covering everything from understanding who customers are (audience data) to creating ads (creative tooling) and showing them to people (activation channels).

Other major technology companies are also adapting to this trend: Adobe is expanding beyond its creative tools, and Microsoft is trying to become more relevant to marketing leaders (CMOs) by connecting its cloud services with AI.
question: The article highlights that marketing agencies are now choosing partners who can connect all parts of their work, from understanding customers to showing ads, rather than just picking the 'smartest' individual tools. In your own work, where do you see opportunities to connect different tasks or systems to create a more complete and effective workflow, instead of relying on separate, isolated tools?
